key_insight: Vitriflow makes melt-quench amorphous-structure generation explicit and material-specific by linking numerical settings, thermal protocol, screening, and convergence into one calibrated decision chain.
engineering_relevance: This gives computational materials engineers a reproducible way to build and compare amorphous ensembles without relying on hidden default choices or ad hoc post-processing.
method: Calibrate melt-quench parameters using descriptor-based protocol selection and numerical stability checks.
Apply user-defined artefact screening to separate valid amorphous structures from defects or recrystallised states.
Assess statistical convergence in a material-specific structural descriptor space, with optional DFT refinement for selected systems.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports demonstrations on a-SiO2, a-Si3N4, and a-Sm2O3, including separation of defect-free versus oxygen-bridge-defective silica, DFT-refinement response tracking, and removal of recrystallised Sm2O3 structures.
watch_item: The framework depends on chosen descriptors and screening rules, so performance may vary across materials if those criteria are not well tuned.
paper_title: Vitriflow: calibrated amorphous structure ensembles from melt-quench simulation
